category_sources: Official GNU page describes Zile as a small text editor with Emacs-like editing features.
source_facts.description says Zile is a text editor development kit, while the official GNU page identifies GNU Zile as a lightweight Emacs clone.
display_name_sources: Official GNU page uses the display name GNU Zile.
docs_sources: Official GNU Zile page says documentation is built into the program, with a man page and FAQ installed locally; the homepage is the best official online documentation surface found.
repo_sources: Official GNU page links the Zile Savannah project page for development sources, and Savannah lists the Git repository for zile.git.
source_facts.repo is empty, so repo was determined from official GNU/Savannah sources.
tags_sources: Official GNU page describes Zile as a lightweight Emacs clone with basic editing features.
Savannah project identifies GNU Zile as official GNU software.
source_facts.executables lists zile as a CLI executable.
